_comment: QueryStudio configuration. All values can be overridden by environment variables (see config.py).

paths._comment: Relative paths are resolved from the backend/ directory. Use absolute paths for OpenShift/container deployments.
paths._comment_duckdb_spill_dir: BF-599d/600-cfg (2026-05-31): explicit spill-to-disk path for multi-worker queries that exceed memory_limit. Empty string = derive from duckdb_temp_dir + '/spill'. Override per-pod via DUCKDB_TEMP_DIR env (takes precedence). On Windows dev, falls back to tempfile.gettempdir() + 'duckdb_spill' — never a hardcoded /tmp.

query_engine._comment_max_concurrent_per_user: BF-600-M5 (2026-05-31): default lowered 50 -> 5 to prevent the 100 users x 50 = 5000 in-flight scenario flagged in the OpenShift scale audit. 5 is generous for interactive BQB/CQB usage; batch callers (feed_gen, scheduler) bypass via their own slot manager. Raise this back to 50 if you have a small team and want zero queueing.
query_engine._comment_max_concurrent_global: BF-584 (2026-05-29): hard pod-wide cap. Combined with per-user cap 5, max possible in-flight = min(200, n_users * 5).
query_engine._comment_max_queue_size: BF-584: bursts above max_concurrent_per_user enter the queue. When queue is also full, the endpoint returns 503 with Retry-After=30.
query_engine._comment_queue_wait_timeout: How long a queued query waits for a slot before giving up with 503. 0 disables queueing (legacy 429 behaviour).
query_engine._comment_duckdb_memory_limit_mb: Per-connection DuckDB memory cap (MB). 0 = unlimited (DuckDB auto-detects host RAM). BF-599d takes precedence for multi-worker dispatches: each worker gets pod_mem * 0.7 / n_workers when cgroup is detectable.
query_engine._env_overrides: Every value here is overridable via env vars (see config.py).  BF-599 series adds: QS_AUTOINSTALL_EXTENSIONS=false (BF-599f: OpenShift NFS-prestaged extensions; skip httpfs INSTALL version-check); DUCKDB_TEMP_DIR=<spill path> (BF-599d: spill-to-disk for heavy aggregations); DUCKDB_MEMORY_LIMIT=<e.g. 1500MB> (BF-599d: per-pod override of duckdb_memory_limit_mb).

rest_api._comment: REST connector + shared HTTP SSRF guard (Phase 123-C). Defaults block private/loopback/link-local IPs so an attacker can't pivot internally via a connector URL.
rest_api._comment_allow_private_ips: Set TRUE for on-prem deployments where REST connections (SAP, Trino portal, Starburst portal, internal SOAP) target internal hostnames that resolve to RFC1918 IPs. Cloud-metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254, metadata.google.internal) remain BLOCKED unconditionally.

s3._comment_handler_cache: TTL for cached S3StorageHandler instances (seconds). 2700s = 45min, STS tokens expire at 60min.
s3._comment_parallel_download_workers: Thread count for parallel S3 file downloads (presigned pre-download + boto3 proxy pre-download). BF-416 — bumped 8 → 32 to match the code default; the old 8 was a serial bottleneck on 1000+ file scans through corporate proxies. Override per-pod via QS_S3_PARALLEL_WORKERS env var. For air-gapped / very-low-bandwidth links, lower this to 4-8.
s3._comment_predownload_max_disk_gb: BF-416 — refuse the boto3 pre-download up front when projected total bytes (HEAD-summed) exceed this cap.  Replaces the old behaviour of running out of disk mid-query.  0 = no cap (legacy).  Set to e.g. 50 for a 100GB PVC to leave headroom.
s3._comment_predownload_skip_threshold: BF-416 — when len(post-pruning files) <= this, skip pre-download and let DuckDB httpfs read directly through the proxy.  For tiny scans (~tens of files) the per-file local-detour overhead exceeds the savings.  0 = always pre-download (legacy).  Try 50 if you have many small queries.
s3._comment_download_retries: Per-file retry attempts for transient S3 errors (503, SlowDown, throttling, network blips). Subprocess uses botocore adaptive mode; streaming uses exponential backoff (1s-8s).
s3._comment_download_timeout: Per-file read timeout (seconds) for boto3 S3 downloads. Applies to subprocess path via BotoConfig(read_timeout=N). Override via QS_S3_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT env var.
s3._comment_min_free_disk_gb: Minimum free disk space (GB) required before starting S3 pre-download. Fails fast with RuntimeError if below threshold. Override via QS_DOWNLOAD_MIN_FREE_GB env var.
s3._comment_max_pool_connections: boto3/urllib3 connection pool size per S3 host. Default 64 — must be >= the highest concurrent thread count that uses the shared client (column-stats scan is capped at 128 workers per scan; cross-conn parallel = 32; subprocess pre-download = 32). When exceeded, urllib3 logs 'Connection pool is full, discarding connection' and silently re-establishes — slow + warning spam. Bump higher if you raise max_workers anywhere.
s3._comment_duckdb_union_by_name_iceberg: BF-PRUNE-PERF — when true, DuckDB reads the parquet footer of EVERY file at CREATE VIEW time to compute the column union (~50s on 3000-file Iceberg tables). FALSE is the safe default for Iceberg because writers project every file to the table schema. Set true only if you have a heterogeneous file set (rare, usually a bug).
s3._comment_duckdb_union_by_name_parquet: BF-PRUNE-PERF — same flag for plain (non-Iceberg) parquet tables. FALSE assumes uniform schema across files in the table prefix (typical when one writer emits the dataset). Set true if you genuinely have files with different column sets (the union view will then include all columns, NULL where missing).

s3_local_cache._comment: BF-257 — persistent NFS cache of S3 DATA files (the actual parquet bytes).  Iceberg data files are content-addressable / immutable so a path-keyed cache with no ETag check is safe.  Equivalent to Trino split-cache or Snowflake warehouse cache.
s3_local_cache._comment_when_to_enable: Turn ON if you want repeat queries on the same data to read from local NFS instead of S3.  REQUIRED if you also want BF-301 metadata_sync_predownload to do anything (predownload writes here).
s3_local_cache._comment_warning: When enabled + metadata_sync_predownload_enabled=true, the sync loop will pull lots of parquet data files into this directory — can be massive (hours through corporate proxy).  Make sure max_size_gb + min_free_disk_gb fit your PVC.
s3_local_cache._comment_enabled: Master switch.  When false, queries fetch S3 files to a per-query temp dir that's deleted right after.  When true, files persist across queries and the eviction job tidies up.
s3_local_cache._comment_cache_dir: Filesystem path the backend pod can write to.  MUST be absolute and writable.  Typically a PVC mount, e.g. /usr/local/querystudioapi/nfs/data/s3-cache.  Leave empty when enabled=false.
s3_local_cache._comment_max_size_gb: Soft cap in GB.  When exceeded, the LRU eviction job removes oldest-accessed files first.
s3_local_cache._comment_evict_interval_minutes: How often the LRU eviction job runs.
s3_local_cache._comment_min_free_disk_gb: Refuse new writes if free disk falls below this floor.  Fail-fast guard against a runaway sync filling the disk.

iceberg._comment: BF-296/298/299/301 — Iceberg metadata cache + background sync.  After warm-up, query pruning runs LOCALLY against cached metadata in the QueryStudio DB; S3 is contacted only for the surviving data files (typically 12 of 4000+).
iceberg._comment_storage: Metadata is cached in the QueryStudio DB tables iceberg_snapshot_cache + iceberg_manifest_cache + watched_iceberg_tables.  NOT in s3_local_cache.cache_dir — that directory only holds DATA files (the parquet bytes).
iceberg._comment_recommended_setup: For production: leave snapshot_cache_enabled + metadata_sync_enabled at true (default).  Leave metadata_sync_predownload_enabled at FALSE — the metadata cache alone gives the big query-time win without the disk + bandwidth cost of pre-downloading data files.
iceberg._comment_metadata_cache_ttl_seconds: BF-273 — TTL for the resolved metadata.json filename per table (avoids repeatedly listing the metadata/ prefix on S3).  Independent from the BF-296 snapshot pointer TTL below.
iceberg._comment_cache_pointer_verification: BF-503 (2026-05-28) — when true, even L1 metadata-key CACHE HITS perform a cheap version-hint.text GET (~5-10ms per S3 round-trip) to verify the live Iceberg pointer hasn't advanced since the cache was populated.  If it has, the cache entry is invalidated and we re-resolve the latest metadata key.  Default true for correctness; set false only if per-query latency cost is unacceptable and 1-minute staleness is acceptable.
iceberg._comment_use_pyiceberg: BF-510 (2026-05-28) — when true, get_iceberg_data_files delegates manifest walk + pruning to the official Apache pyiceberg library instead of the hand-rolled path.  Routes date/timestamp/bucket/truncate/spec-evolution handling through pyiceberg's native Transform.project + BoundPredicate.eval.  Default false during rollout; flip to true after operator validation in staging.  On equality-delete (v2) tables that pyiceberg 0.11.1 doesn't yet support, we fail-open to the hand-rolled path automatically.
iceberg._comment_max_files_per_query: BF-316 — hard cap on files per query (after pruning).  When the WHERE narrows to MORE files than this, the engine raises an actionable error instead of silently downloading thousands of parquets.  0 = no cap (default).  Set to e.g. 200 in production so accidental 'scan everything' queries get rejected with a clear message about which filter columns lack min/max stats.
iceberg._comment_metadata_count_warn_threshold: Log a WARNING when an Iceberg table has more than this many metadata files — admin should run expire_snapshots / remove_orphan_files in Spark or Trino.
iceberg._comment_snapshot_cache_enabled: BF-296/298 — master switch for the L1 pointer + L2 contents + L3 per-manifest caches.  Set false to roll back the entire feature.  Iceberg snapshots are immutable so cache poisoning is impossible — the only invalidation case is missing a NEWER snapshot, bounded by snapshot_pointer_ttl_hours.
iceberg._comment_snapshot_pointer_ttl_hours: How long the L1 pointer (RAM mapping of table → snapshot_id) is trusted before re-reading metadata.json from S3.  Lower (e.g. 1) for tables with frequent writes; raise for mostly-static tables.  Admins can also click 'Refresh now' to invalidate immediately.
iceberg._comment_metadata_sync_enabled: BF-299 — master switch for the background sync loop that proactively reconciles every watched table.  When false, the cache still works but populates lazily on user queries.
iceberg._comment_metadata_sync_autostart: BF-MGD-BG-JOBS-OPT-IN — admin opts in via /admin/background-jobs → Start.  When false the sync loop is REGISTERED (visible in admin UI) but does NOT run on boot.  Admin pause/start state persists across pod restarts via background_job_states.
iceberg._comment_metadata_sync_startup_delay_sec: Seconds after backend startup before the FIRST sync tick fires.  Raise to 60+ if you want the backend to be fully ready (health probes stable) before any S3 chatter begins.
iceberg._comment_metadata_sync_interval_minutes: How often the sync ticks.  Each tick: list watched tables, for each one read metadata.json + reconcile manifests + (optionally) predownload top-N data files.
iceberg._comment_metadata_sync_max_per_cycle: Max number of tables reconciled per tick — bounds load on big deployments.  If you have 500 tables, set ~50 so each tick is bounded but the rotation completes within an hour.
iceberg._comment_metadata_sync_predownload_enabl: BF-301 — pre-fetch the top-N largest DATA FILES (parquet bytes) into the s3_local_cache during sync, so the user's FIRST query reads from local disk instead of S3.  REQUIRES s3_local_cache.enabled=true.  Default OFF because downloads can be massive (50 files × 100+ MB through corporate proxy = hours).  RECOMMENDED: leave OFF — the metadata cache alone gives the big query-time win.  Turn on only if you have plenty of NFS space + fast S3 access.
iceberg._comment_metadata_sync_predownload_max_f: Per table per tick.  Files are picked by record_count desc (largest = most likely queried).
iceberg._comment_metadata_sync_predownload_max_g: Per table per tick byte cap.  Sizes are head_object'd from S3 before download to honour the cap accurately.
iceberg._comment_column_stats_sync_enabled: BF-COL-STATS — extract per-file column min/max bounds from parquet footers and cache them in iceberg_column_stats_supplement so pruning can use them when the iceberg writer didn't track bounds for a filter column.  ~16-256 KB per file footer read.  Disabled by default — admin opt-in once disk + bandwidth budget is confirmed.  When ON, runs as part of metadata_sync_loop AFTER the predownload step.
iceberg._comment_column_stats_sync_workers: Parallel boto3 footer reads per table.  Raise on fast S3 + plenty of CPU; lower if backend pod is small.
iceberg._comment_column_stats_sync_max_files_per: 0 = unlimited.  Caps per-tick boto3 traffic for huge tables.
iceberg._comment_column_stats_max_workers_cap: Admin-API per-request cap when an operator manually triggers a column-stats scan from /admin → Iceberg Metadata Sync.
iceberg._comment_snapshot_cache_max_entries: BF-iceberg-cache-lru — max rows kept in IcebergSnapshotCache before LRU eviction.  Scheduler runs every 6h to drop the least-recently-accessed entries beyond this cap.  200 snapshots × 1 KB each = 200 KB.  Raise if you query many distinct tables; lower to save DB space.
iceberg._comment_manifest_cache_max_entries: Same as above but for IcebergManifestCache.  5000 manifests × 5 KB each = 25 MB.
iceberg._comment_auto_supplement_on_query_miss: BF-547 (2026-05-28) — auto-trigger column-stats supplement scan when a query needs file-level pruning on columns whose bounds are missing from both the manifest AND the supplement.  Fire-and-forget background thread: the CURRENT query keeps degraded pruning, the NEXT query benefits from a populated supplement.  Throttled 10 min per (bucket, prefix).  Set false to disable.
iceberg._comment_s3_bloom_check_enabled: BF-548 (2026-05-29) — S3-side bloom filter pre-skip.  When true, the iceberg reader issues a 256 KB suffix-range GET per file to read its parquet bloom filter footer, then SKIPS files where bloom proves absence for any equality predicate.  Off by default — net-negative for queries WITHOUT bloom-friendly equality predicates.  Flip on for connections with high-cardinality equality filters (account_id, customer_id, sap_company_code).  Bloom can never produce false negatives so this is correctness-safe.
iceberg._comment_metadata_key_db_cache_max_age_s: BF-511 (2026-05-28) — hard ceiling on how long an IcebergMetadataKeyCache row may serve without S3 corroboration.  Rows are verified against version-hint.text on every hit; if verification SUCCEEDS, row is served regardless of age.  If verification FAILS (V1 table, transient S3 error, cache_pointer_verification disabled), row is served ONLY if younger than this max-age.  Set 0 to disable the DB cache layer entirely.
iceberg._comment_metadata_key_background_refresh: BF-512 (2026-05-28) — proactive background sweep that re-resolves every non-sentinel IcebergMetadataKeyCache row against S3 on the configured interval, independent of query traffic.  Ensures rows touched by NO queries still get refreshed.  Set false to disable; BF-511's hard max-age still caps stale-row blast radius.
How often the background refresh sweep fires.  Interval is read at scheduler init — changes require a backend restart.

Rows processed per tick (0 = unlimited).  Useful when the cache holds thousands of rows and admin wants bounded per-tick S3 cost.  Rows sorted oldest-first so stale-risk is processed before fresh rows.

iceberg_mor._comment: BF-273 — Iceberg V2 merge-on-read. Set enabled=true if your tables have V2 delete files (UPDATE / DELETE / MERGE).

scan_strategy._comment: ScanStrategist (2026-06-01) — single source of truth for per-scan execution decisions (file_order, max_workers, footer prewarm, DuckDB pragmas, boto3 pool).  Replaces scattered BF-535 hard-coded smallest-first + multi_worker.max_workers reads at 4 call sites.
scan_strategy._comment_recommendation: Production default: leave 'profile' at 'auto' so per-call heuristics pick bulk_scan / interactive / low_latency.  Per-profile overrides go under 'profiles.<name>.<key>'.  Disable prewarm globally via 'prewarm.enabled=false' (useful when corporate proxy makes HEAD calls slower than the parallel scan they're trying to warm).
scan_strategy._comment_profile: auto | bulk_scan | interactive | low_latency.  bulk_scan = largest-first + 16 workers + parallel footer prewarm + DuckDB 8 threads + boto3 pool 64 (tuned for the 3000-file feed-gen case).  interactive = smallest-first + 4 workers + no prewarm (cache likely warm).  low_latency = published-API; cache-first + size-balanced + no blocking prewarm.  auto picks per call.
scan_strategy._comment_default_file_order: Fallback ordering for callers that haven't been migrated to pass an explicit ScanPlan yet.  Default 'manifest_order' (no implicit sort) preserves DuckDB parallel-scanner behaviour.  Admin can flip to 'largest_first' to globally fix bulk scans even before individual callers are migrated.  Other values: smallest_first, size_balanced.

profiles._comment: Per-profile override map.  Each value is a partial ScanPlan dict that overlays the built-in profile defaults.  Unset keys inherit.  Examples: {"bulk_scan":{"max_workers":32}} bumps bulk_scan workers; {"interactive":{"prewarm_footers":true}} forces prewarm for interactive.

prewarm._comment: Footer prewarm — issues parallel HEAD requests for every parquet file BEFORE the DuckDB scan opens them.  Helps when boto3 connection pool is cold or files live behind a slow VPC endpoint.  Hurts when the proxy makes HEAD calls block longer than the parallel scan they're trying to warm.  Read on every prewarm call so admin can toggle without restart.
prewarm._comment_enabled: Global kill switch.  Set false to disable ALL footer prewarm (per-query AND the background prewarm service).  Per the scan_strategy.py:425 doc: 'Useful when the proxy makes prewarm a net negative (HEAD calls block longer than the parallel-scan they're trying to warm).'
prewarm._comment_per_head_timeout_s: Per-HEAD timeout in seconds.  Default 5.  Raise to 10-30 on slow proxies where the default causes false-positive timeouts.

spark_connect._comment: Spark Connect (gRPC) — optional offload path for very large queries.  Disabled by default; flip 'enabled' AFTER the Spark Connect server pod is deployed and reachable.  See frontend/public/docs/spark-connect-deployment.html for K8s manifests.
spark_connect._comment_server_url: Format: sc://host:port for in-cluster; sc://host:port/;use_ssl=true for TLS; sc://route.apps.cluster:443/;use_ssl=true for OpenShift Route + TLS passthrough.  Append ;token=... for bearer auth (or use 'system_account' to pull from the encrypted System Account store).
spark_connect._comment_token: BF-267 — plain-text bearer token (dev only).  In production set 'system_account' to a System Account name whose 'token' field carries the secret.
spark_connect._comment_system_account: Name of a System Account whose token field carries the Spark Connect bearer.  Same pattern proxy / aws_portal use.  When both 'token' and 'system_account' are blank we don't append ;token= so Spark Connect uses its default (none / mTLS / k8s SA token).
spark_connect._comment_row_threshold: Queries projected to return >= this many rows route to Spark Connect; smaller queries stay on DuckDB.  Set 1 temporarily during testing to force every query through Spark.  Restore to ~10000000 (or your prod threshold) after.
spark_connect._comment_circuit_breaker_failure_thresho: Number of consecutive Spark Connect failures before the circuit breaker opens and ALL queries fall back to DuckDB.  Reset via POST /api/admin/spark-connect/circuit-breaker/reset after fixing the server.

s3_readers._comment: BF-428 — pluggable S3 read strategies.  6 readers + cost-based router.  Master switches OFF by default — operators flip 'integration_enabled' (AWS) and 'presigned_router_enabled' (on-prem) to activate.  See frontend/public/docs/bf-428-readers.html for the operator runbook.
s3_readers._comment_default: auto | direct_httpfs | full_predownload | pyarrow_s3fs | smart_predownload | s3_select | athena.  'auto' uses the cost-based router.
s3_readers._comment_auto_route_enabled: Phase F kill-switch — set false to use the simple proxy-on/off heuristic instead of the router.
s3_readers._comment_integration_enabled: AWS bridge (BF-428K) — when true, the router can route AWS-region queries through smart_predownload / pyarrow_s3fs / s3_select.  Off by default; flip on after staging validation.
s3_readers._comment_presigned_router_enabled: On-prem bridge (BF-429) — when true, the router can route presigned-URL queries (Dell ECS / MinIO style) through pyarrow_s3fs / smart_predownload.
s3_readers._comment_per_connection: Per-connection pinned reader: {connection_id_str: reader_name}.  Overrides the auto route for THIS connection only.
s3_readers._comment_athena_database: Required to enable the Athena reader.  Leave blank if your team doesn't have an Athena account.
s3_readers._comment_athena_output_location: Required for Athena — s3://bucket/path/ for query results.
s3_readers._comment_cost_overrides: Per-reader cost-model overrides.  Use after watching /api/admin/s3-readers/telemetry for a week to tune defaults to your workload.

database._comment_postgresql: Set type=postgresql and postgresql_url (or QUERYSTUDIO_DATABASE_POSTGRESQL_URL env var). Falls back to SQLite if PG is unreachable.
database._comment_oracle: Set type=oracle and fill oracle_* fields (or use QUERYSTUDIO_DATABASE_* env vars) to use Oracle as the metadata store instead of SQLite.

auth._comment: User authentication. type='none' (default, no login) | 'local' (config-based users) | 'ad' (Active Directory / LDAP).
auth._comment_local_users: Format: user1:pass1,user2:pass2. Only used when type=local.
auth._comment_user_roles: Format: user1:admin,user2:analyst,user3:viewer. Overrides AD group-based role.
auth._comment_ad_use_ssl: Set true for ldaps:// connections (port 636). Auto-detected from the ldaps:// URL scheme.
auth._comment_ad_tls_validate: Validate the AD server's TLS certificate. Set false for self-signed certs in dev (Option A quick unblock). Leave true in prod + set ad_tls_ca_file.
auth._comment_ad_tls_check_hostname: Verify cert CN/SAN matches ad_server hostname. Default false because AD certs are issued to the DC FQDN (e.g. dc01.corp.com), not the domain name (corp.com). Set true only if your cert has SANs matching ad_server.
auth._comment_ad_tls_ca_file: Absolute path to a PEM CA bundle for the AD server cert. Empty = use the OS default trust store. In OpenShift, mount a Secret at /app/certs/ad-ca/ca.crt and set this to that path.
auth._comment_ad: Set type=ad and fill ad_* fields (or QUERYSTUDIO_AUTH_* env vars). Groups are comma-separated AD group names mapped to roles.

scheduler._comment: Automated cleanup intervals and retention windows.
scheduler._comment_cleanup: Delete unregistered temp result files older than N hours (runs every 30 min). Default: 1
scheduler._comment_retention: Delete query execution history + result parquet files older than N days (runs daily). Default: 30

storage._comment: Disk space management for the downloads folder.
storage._comment_expiry: How many days a Download Centre file stays 'available' before expiring. Default: 7
storage._comment_quota: Soft quota for the downloads folder in MB. Oldest files are expired first when exceeded. Default: 10240 (10 GB)
storage._comment_cache: Max size of the query result cache in MB before LRU eviction. Default: 2048 (2 GB)

cors._comment: For production, replace * with your actual domain: https://querystudio.example.com

cache._comment: Query result cache â€” served from disk parquet, zero extra servers required.

result_cache._comment: BF-382 (Option D) — repeat-query result cache. SHA256(sql+params+file-fingerprint) keys, L1 in-memory + L2 on-disk parquet. ENABLED by default — fail-open at every error path, zero overhead when no query hits.

multi_worker._comment: BF-383 (Option F) — split large queries across N parallel DuckDB workers IN THE SAME POD. ENABLED by default. Only fires when query is splittable + files ≥ min_files_to_split. JOIN/DISTINCT/window functions auto-fall-back to single-worker (unless allow_broadcast_join=true).
multi_worker._comment_allow_broadcast_join: Phase 141/143 — when TRUE, JOIN queries also get split: the big side's files are split across workers, every worker reads the full small-table file list (broadcast). HAVING is now supported (Phase 143-B). Refuses unsafe shapes automatically (DISTINCT / WINDOW / WITH / ORDER-without-LIMIT / FULL OUTER + small side with > broadcast_max_small_files) and falls back to single-worker.  2026-06-03 flipped to TRUE by default — operator report of 3000-file JOIN queries silently routing to single-worker because this guard was never flipped on.
multi_worker._comment_broadcast_max_rows: Maximum row count for a table to be considered the 'small' (broadcast) side. Above this threshold the table is treated as 'big' — if both sides are big the query refuses to broadcast (use partition-aligned or Spark Connect instead).
multi_worker._comment_broadcast_max_small_files: Maximum file count on the small side. Above this cap broadcast-JOIN refuses (each worker would scan too many small-side files, killing the throughput win).
multi_worker._comment_allow_partition_aligned_join: Phase 144 — when TRUE, INNER JOIN on co-partitioned tables (both tables share the same Hive partition columns AND values) splits per partition tuple: each worker gets ALL files from BOTH tables for ONE partition tuple, runs the JOIN locally, merger UNIONs. This is the big × big JOIN path that does NOT need Spark Connect. Correctness contract: related rows (same JOIN key) MUST share partition values on both sides — if your writer doesn't guarantee this (e.g. partitioned by ingest_date not transaction_date), DO NOT enable. Refused for LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER JOINs (would silently drop rows from non-shared partitions). See docs/partition-aligned-join.md.

multi_pod._comment: BF-384 (Option G) â€” distribute chunks across SEPARATE pods over HTTP. Workers discovered via static URLs, K8s headless service DNS, or worker_registry. Falls back to in-pod F when no pods reachable. PRODUCTION: set shared_secret via QS_WORKER_SECRET env var, NOT in config.json.

engine_router._comment: BF-385 â€” Engine Router picks single / multi_worker / multi_pod / spark_connect per query. Empty rules = use built-in DEFAULT_RULES. Set rules to customize routing policy.

email._comment: SMTP settings for schedule notifications. Leave host empty to disable email.
email._comment_env: Override with QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_HOST, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_PORT, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_USER, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_PASSWORD, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_FROM_ADDRESS, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_USE_TLS

chatbot._comment: Column classification patterns for the AI Assistant chip suggestions.
chatbot._comment_usage: Patterns are matched case-insensitively as substrings of column names. Add your organisation-specific column names or partial keywords to extend auto-detection.
chatbot._comment_numeric: Columns where SUM / AVG / MIN / MAX are meaningful.
chatbot._comment_category: Columns used for GROUP BY, COUNT BY, or DISTINCT analysis.
chatbot._comment_date: Columns used for trend / time-series analysis.
chatbot._comment_id: ID / key columns â€” SUM/AVG not meaningful, only COUNT/FILTER.

faq._comment: Keyword â†’ answer map for the AI Assistant. Keys are matched case-insensitively as substrings of the user's message. Add organisation-specific Q&A here.
faq.what is querystudio: QueryStudio is a self-service data query and analytics platform. It lets you query databases, S3 files, and uploaded data â€” then visualise results as charts, pivot tables, and dashboards.
faq.who built querystudio: QueryStudio is built in-house as an internal analytics platform.
faq.contact support: Raise a support ticket with your IT / Data Engineering team, or email the platform owner.
faq.what data sources: QueryStudio connects to: Amazon S3 (Parquet/CSV/JSON), Snowflake, Databricks, Trino, Oracle, and local file uploads (CSV, Excel, Parquet, JSON).
faq.what file formats: Supported upload formats: CSV, Excel (.xlsx), Parquet, JSON. Results can be downloaded as CSV, Excel, or Parquet.
faq.how many rows: The default query limit is 1,000 rows. Admins can raise this per role. Use streaming mode for large datasets.
faq.data retention: Query execution history is kept for 30 days by default. Download files expire after 7 days.
faq.is my data secure: QueryStudio runs within your organisation's network. Credentials are encrypted, queries run server-side, and role-based access controls limit what each user can see.

glossary._comment: Business term â†’ technical column/table/SQL fragment. Applied automatically when NL-to-SQL runs. Teach the chatbot your org's data vocabulary.
glossary._comment_examples: Map a business word to an actual column name, table name, or SQL snippet. E.g. 'revenue': 'sale_amount' means 'show revenue' becomes 'show sale_amount'.

suggestion_corrections._comment: Smart aggregation and join correction learning. The system learns from user overrides.

partition_cache._comment: Two-tier partition cache: L1 in-memory (fast) + L2 SQLite (survives restarts). TTLs in seconds.

report_catalog._comment: Phase 58: Unified report catalog with certification, sharing, and embedding.

business_portal._comment: Phase 59: Business Portal landing page settings.

proxy._comment: Corporate proxy for outbound connections (boto3, DuckDB httpfs, httpx, requests). On-prem S3 does NOT need proxy — leave enabled=false.
proxy._comment_url: Base proxy URL (e.g. http://proxy:8080). For auth, use system_account instead of embedding credentials here.
proxy._comment_host: Proxy hostname — auto-builds http://host:port if url is empty.
proxy._comment_system_account: Name of a System Account (Admin > System Accounts, type=proxy) for proxy authentication. Credentials are encrypted in the DB — never stored as plain text.
proxy._comment_no_proxy: Comma-separated bypass list. On-prem S3 endpoints should be added here (e.g. 's3.internal.corp.com,.internal.com')
proxy._comment_apply_to_boto3: Inject proxy into boto3/botocore S3 clients. Required for: S3 file listing, schema refresh, iceberg metadata, and boto3 pre-download fallback. Must be true if apply_to_duckdb is true.
proxy._comment_apply_to_duckdb: When true AND proxy is enabled: DuckDB queries use boto3 pre-download fallback (downloads S3 parquet via boto3 to local temp, DuckDB reads local files). Needed because DuckDB httpfs (libcurl) gets HTTP 503 from corporate proxies that reject its auth negotiation. boto3 (urllib3) handles proxy auth correctly. Parallel download with 8 workers, adaptive retries, disk space guard.
proxy._comment_apply_to_httpx: Inject proxy into httpx and requests clients. Used by: REST API connector, NL chatbot, external API calls.

background_jobs_autostart._comment: BF-MGD-BG-JOBS-OPT-IN — admin opts in to optional background jobs.  When false the job is REGISTERED (visible in /admin/background-jobs) but DOES NOT run on backend startup; admin clicks Start to begin.  Pause/start state then persists across restarts via background_job_states DB table.  Set true here OR flip via /admin/app-config at runtime.

published_api._comment: Published Views consumer-facing API (/api/v1/*). Runs as a separate pod when publishedApi.enabled=true in Helm.
published_api._comment_base_url: External HTTPS URL of the Published API (e.g. https://querystudio-published-api.apps.cluster.example.com). Used for webhook callbacks and API documentation. Leave empty when running in shared mode (same pod as main API).
published_api._comment_max_concurrent: Max concurrent Published API executions. Default: 50
published_api._comment_default_timeout: Per-request timeout in seconds. Default: 1800 (30 min)
published_api._comment_max_queue: Max queued Published API requests before rejecting. Default: 200

user_drafts._comment: Phase 142 — Server-side draft persistence. Replaces per-component localStorage. Every page that has user input persists drafts here so refresh / cache clear / device switch / pod restart never loses work. Drafts are per-user and DB-encrypted (same posture as queries, connections, sessions).
user_drafts._comment_enabled: Master switch. When false, /api/user/drafts/* returns 503 and the frontend falls back to in-memory-only (refresh loses work).
user_drafts._comment_retention_days: Daily cleanup deletes drafts older than this. 0 = keep forever.
user_drafts._comment_per_draft_max_mb: Maximum size of one draft. PUTs above this get HTTP 413. Typical typed SQL is <50 KB.
user_drafts._comment_per_user_max_mb: Maximum total size per user across ALL drafts.
user_drafts._comment_cleanup_interval_hours: How often the background cleanup job runs.

smart_cancel._comment: Phase 141 — Auto-cancellation of stale executions. The cancel only fires when BOTH the client heartbeat AND the engine progress timestamp are stale. Tune these if your corporate proxy idle-timeout is unusually long or short.
smart_cancel._comment_client_stale_seconds: Cancel ONLY when no client heartbeat for this many seconds (default 180s = 3 min). Higher = more tolerant of network blips / corporate-proxy WS drops. Lower = faster cleanup when users close browser.
smart_cancel._comment_engine_progress_grace_seconds: AND when the engine itself has emitted no progress for this many seconds (default 120s = 2 min). An actively-scanning DuckDB query stamps progress every ~15s, so this only fires on genuinely-stuck queries.
smart_cancel._comment_check_interval_seconds: How often the background loop scans for stale executions. Default 30s.

row_security._comment: Phase 141 — Row & column-level security (Lake Formation equivalent). Policies stored in row_level_policies + column_policies + policy_bindings tables. SQL is rewritten by sqlglot before execution. See docs/row-security.md.
row_security._comment_enabled: Master switch. When false the rewriter is a no-op regardless of policy rows.
row_security._comment_default_mode: Used when a policy row has no explicit enforcement value. Options: rewrite | view | native_lf | audit_only. Start in audit_only to validate policies against real traffic without affecting results.
row_security._comment_fail_open: If a rewrite raises (parse error, unsupported construct), fall back to ORIGINAL sql (fail-open) instead of refusing to execute (fail-closed). Default false = fail-closed which is the secure default.
row_security._comment_audit_retention_days: Daily cleanup deletes row_security_audit rows older than this. 0 = keep forever (not recommended).
row_security._comment_cache_resolver_seconds: TTL for the (principal, connection, table) policy lookup cache. 0 = no cache. Higher = lower DB load but slower propagation of policy edits.
row_security._comment_lake_formation_sync_enabled: When true and a connection has aws_assume_role + lake_formation_enabled, periodically fetch LF data filters and upsert into row_level_policies with source='lake_formation'.
row_security._comment_materialize_views_enabled: Option B — eagerly CREATE VIEW <t>_secured at session start. Cheaper at query time but requires per-session DuckDB state.
row_security._comment_max_filter_sql_chars: Maximum length of filter_sql allowed on a policy. Defensive guard against accidentally-huge expressions.

feed_generation._comment: Feed file generation defaults. Use FORWARD SLASHES even on Windows — Python's os.path handles both, and forward slashes avoid the JSON-escape-character pitfall (single backslash + 'c' = invalid JSON escape). On Linux/OpenShift these resolve as-is.
feed_generation._comment_allowed_roots: Windows dev paths. On Linux/OpenShift these resolve to non-existent paths and are silently ignored by the validator (no security impact). Admins can override at runtime via Admin → System Settings → feed_generation.local_destination_allowed_roots without a pod restart.
